questions.subject: Economy
questions.prompt: In the context of global trade governance, Free Trade Agreements (FTAs) are often described as an 'exception' to which of the following principles of the World Trade Organisation (WTO)?
questions.explanation: The MFN principle requires a WTO member to treat all other members equally. However, FTAs allow member countries to grant preferential treatment (lower tariffs) to their trade partners within the agreement without extending those same benefits to all other WTO members, thus acting as a legal exception to the MFN rule.
questions.takeaway: FTAs are a sanctioned exception to the WTO's MFN principle, allowing for preferential trade liberalization between specific partners.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: FTAs act as an exception to the Most Favoured Nation (MFN) principle of the World Trade Organisation (WTO).

questions.subject: Economy
questions.prompt: Which of the following is the most advanced level of economic integration, characterized by the establishment of common economic policies and shared institutions among member states?
questions.explanation: An Economic Union is the deepest level of integration in the trade hierarchy. It includes the features of a Common Market (free movement of goods, services, labour, and capital) but adds the requirement of harmonized or common economic policies and the creation of supranational institutions.
questions.takeaway: Economic Union represents the highest stage of trade integration, involving policy and institutional harmonization.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Economic Union is the deepest level of integration, featuring common economic policies and institutions.

questions.subject: Economy
questions.prompt: In the context of international trade negotiations, the term 'Early Harvest Scheme' is best described as:

options.text: A mechanism to provide immediate subsidies to the agricultural sector before a trade deal is signed.
A precursor to a Free Trade Agreement where specific products are identified for early tariff liberalization.
A temporary suspension of trade barriers during a global economic crisis to boost demand.
The final stage of an Economic Union where all technical standards are harmonized.

questions.explanation: An Early Harvest Scheme (EHS) is a tactical precursor to a full FTA or other trade agreements. It allows the negotiating parties to identify certain products for immediate tariff reduction or elimination to build confidence before concluding a comprehensive agreement.
questions.takeaway: Early Harvest Schemes serve as confidence-building precursors to more comprehensive trade agreements.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Early Harvest Scheme is a precursor to an FTA and other trade agreements.

questions.subject: Economy
questions.prompt: Which of the following features specifically distinguishes a 'Common Market' from a 'Customs Union'?

options.text: The elimination of all internal tariffs and quotas between member nations.
The adoption of a unified tariff policy toward countries outside the agreement.
The removal of restrictions on the cross-border movement of labor and capital.
The integration of monetary and fiscal policies under a single central bank.

questions.explanation: While both a Customs Union and a Common Market involve zero internal duties and common external tariffs (Options A and B), the defining distinction is that a Common Market also allows for the free movement of labor and capital (factors of production). Option D refers to an Economic Union.
questions.takeaway: The free movement of labor and capital is the key factor that elevates a Customs Union to a Common Market.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Custom Union involves zero duty trade among member countries and common tariffs against the rest of the world. Common Market allows free movement of labour and capital, along with harmonized technical standards.
